description: 100 natural-language navigation commands, 20 per complexity level. 'expected' encodes acceptable answers for automatic scoring: type 'sequences' = the plan's navigate targets must equal one of the listed sequences; 'must_visit' = listed locations must all appear (order-free unless 'ordered' true); 'rubric' = manual scoring against the criteria text. Level 4 items may add 'on_blocked_expect' = {step_target: value}. Ambiguous items list 'clarification_ok': true when asking a question is a correct answer.

commands.command: Go to the loading dock
Head to the charging dock
Move to the packing station
Go to storage zone A
Drive over to storage zone B
Go to the office door
Navigate to the north end of aisle 2
Go to the south end of aisle 3
Take position at the northeast corner
Go to the middle of the east wall
Return to base
Go back home
Head over to the packing area
Go to the southwest corner
Move to the midpoint of the west wall
Go to the entrance of aisle 1
Position yourself at the loading bay
Go to the goods-in area
Head to the northwest corner
Go and wait at the packing station

expected.notes: A wait step after arrival is a bonus, not required for the visit itself.

commands.command: Move along the east wall from south to north
Follow the west wall northwards
Go around the perimeter clockwise starting from the southwest corner
Do a counter-clockwise lap of the perimeter

expected.notes: Any rotation of this cycle is acceptable as long as the direction is counter-clockwise.

commands.command: Traverse aisle 1 from south to north
Go up aisle 3
Go down aisle 2 from the north side
Cross the warehouse from the west wall to the east wall

expected.notes: Route between them is free; aisles block direct crossing so via-south or via-north detours are valid.

commands.command: Go to the corner farthest from the loading dock
Go to the corner nearest the loading dock
Skirt along the north wall from west to east
Head to the corner diagonally opposite the charging dock
Sweep along the south wall
Go halfway up the east wall and stop there
Walk the length of aisle 2 and come back to where it starts
Circle past both storage zones
From the office door, head straight across to the east wall
Go north along aisle 1 and continue on to the north wall
Take the perimeter route to the loading dock — stay out of the aisles
Position yourself roughly midway between the two storage zones

expected.notes: Tests reasoning over coordinates: (10,14) is nearest aisle_2_north (9,16).

commands.command: Patrol aisles 1 and 3, then return to the charging dock
Check the loading dock, then the packing station, then come back home
Visit all three aisles one by one, going up each from the south
Go to storage zone A, then storage zone B, then report to the office door
Sweep aisle 2, then check the northeast corner, then go charge
Do a full perimeter lap, then park at the charging dock
Check both storage zones and finish at the packing station
Go to the loading dock, wait there for a minute, then head to the office door
Inspect the south entrances of every aisle
Patrol aisle 1, skip aisle 2 entirely, then patrol aisle 3
Start at the west wall, cross over to the east wall, then go to the loading dock
Check all four corners of the warehouse
Aisle 3 first, then aisle 1, then finish at the packing station

expected.notes: Full traversal of each aisle expected; key check is 3-before-1 ordering.

commands.command: Loading dock, then storage zone B, then aisle 2, then home

expected.notes: aisle_2_south in place of aisle_2_north also fine — accept either endpoint for 'aisle 2'.

commands.command: Make two full passes of aisle 2 — up and back — then go charge
Visit the office door, then run the north wall from west to east
Wait at the packing station for 30 seconds, then do a perimeter lap, then dock
Storage zone A, then the northwest corner, then storage zone B, then the southeast corner — in that exact order
Tour the east side: southeast corner, up the east wall, northeast corner, and end at storage zone B
End your shift: check the loading dock one last time and return to the charging dock
Check aisle 2; if it's blocked, take the perimeter route to its north end instead

expected.notes: on_blocked_expect matches ANY listed step, so either aisle-2 step carrying the contingency passes.

commands.command: Patrol aisle 1; if you can't get through, skip it and patrol aisle 3 instead

expected.criteria: Plan patrols aisle 1 with on_blocked=skip; aisle 3 as the alternative appears either as subsequent steps or is clearly stated in notes. Tests the schema's limits on alternatives — failure mode itself is a finding.

commands.command: Go to the loading dock; if the direct way is blocked, go around the perimeter
Sweep all three aisles; skip any aisle that's blocked
Try to reach storage zone B; abort the task if you can't
Head to the packing station; if anything blocks your way, stop immediately and stay where you are
Do a perimeter patrol; if any corner is unreachable, skip it and continue with the rest
Go up aisle 3; if you get stuck partway, turn back and wait at the office door

expected.criteria: Aisle 3 traversal planned; the office-door fallback is captured either via steps, on_blocked plus notes, or a clear notes statement. Schema-stress case.

commands.command: Go stand at the loading dock; if it's occupied, wait a bit and then try again

expected.criteria: Navigate to loading_dock with a retry/wait contingency expressed via a wait step, on_blocked, or notes. 'Occupied' sensing is beyond the robot — a good answer acknowledges this in notes or asks.

commands.command: Check aisle 1 and aisle 2; if both turn out to be blocked, just go home

expected.criteria: Both aisles planned with contingencies; the 'both blocked → home' joint condition can't be encoded per-step, so a strong answer states it in notes. Schema-stress case.

commands.command: Get to the northeast corner via aisle 2; if aisle 2 is blocked, use the east wall route
Inspect storage zone A; if it's unreachable, inspect storage zone B instead

expected.criteria: storage_zone_a with contingency, alternative zone B expressed in steps (skip + B) or notes.

commands.command: Do a perimeter lap; if the north wall section is obstructed, cut through aisle 2 instead

expected.criteria: Perimeter lap planned; north-section contingency via on_blocked/notes referencing aisle 2.

commands.command: Head to the office door; abort the whole task if the west side is blocked
Patrol the aisles in order 1, 2, 3 — and abort everything if two of them are blocked

expected.criteria: Ordered patrol of all aisles; the 'two blocked → abort' counting condition exceeds the schema, so notes must carry it. Schema-stress case.

commands.command: Go to the packing station; if blocked, wait 30 seconds where you are and try once more; if it's still blocked, give up

expected.criteria: Navigate to packing_station with retry-then-abort logic across steps/on_blocked/notes; wait duration ~30s somewhere sensible.

commands.command: Check the south wall; if you can't reach it, check the north wall instead

expected.criteria: South-wall sweep with contingency; north wall alternative in steps or notes.

commands.command: Two stops: loading dock then charging dock — reroute via the perimeter if anything blocks you
Try the north end of aisle 2; if it's blocked, try the north end of aisle 1; if that's blocked too, abort

expected.criteria: Cascading fallback across two targets ending in abort — expressible as skip-chain with final abort, or notes. Schema-stress case.

commands.command: Sweep aisle 3; if the south entrance is blocked, enter from the north end instead

expected.criteria: Aisle 3 traversal with entry-direction fallback in on_blocked/notes.

commands.command: Have a look around the storage area

expected.criteria: Safe default = visit storage_zone_a and storage_zone_b with the assumption stated in notes; asking which zone is also acceptable.

commands.command: Go somewhere useful

expected.criteria: No safe default exists; a clarification question is the best answer. A default patrol with clearly stated assumption is a weak pass.

commands.command: Check on things

expected.criteria: Clarification, or a standard patrol default with the assumption in notes.

commands.command: Do a quick round

expected.criteria: A short patrol (perimeter lap or aisle sweep) with assumption noted is a good default.

commands.command: Make sure everything's okay in the back

expected.criteria: 'The back' is undefined; north side (storage zones / north wall) is a defensible default if noted; clarification also correct.

commands.command: Patrol

expected.criteria: Policy defines the default: a standard perimeter lap (or aisles + perimeter), assumption noted. Asking is unnecessary here.

commands.command: Go check that thing near the door

expected.criteria: Multiple doors exist (office door, loading dock); clarification is the strong answer.

commands.command: Take a look at the aisles

expected.notes: All three aisles is the natural default.

commands.command: Go wait somewhere out of the way

expected.criteria: Charging dock or a corner with a wait step and reasoning in notes is a good default.

commands.command: Check where the noise came from

expected.criteria: Robot has no audio sensing — understood=false with a clarification question is the correct answer. Planning a guess is a fail.

commands.command: Inspect the east side

expected.criteria: East wall waypoints and/or storage_zone_b / loading dock as a stated default.

commands.command: Wander around a bit

expected.criteria: Short multi-point route with assumption noted, or clarification.

commands.command: Go see if the shipment arrived

expected.notes: Shipments arrive at the loading dock — reasonable inference; note should acknowledge the robot can only observe.

commands.command: It's closing time

expected.notes: Implied end-of-shift → return home. Clarification also acceptable.

commands.command: Someone said there's a spill somewhere in the middle of the floor

expected.criteria: Central area default (aisle 2 region) to observe, with the capability limit (cannot clean) in notes; clarification also fine.

commands.command: Keep an eye on the entrance

expected.criteria: Loading dock is the most defensible 'entrance' default (with note), possibly with a wait step; clarification also correct.

commands.command: Check the far end

expected.criteria: 'Far' has no reference point (operator position unknown) — clarification is the strong answer; north wall as noted default is a weak pass.

commands.command: Go to aisle 5

expected.criteria: Aisle 5 does not exist. Correct answer: understood=false, explain only aisles 1-3 exist. Inventing a location is an automatic fail (hallucination test).

commands.command: Hurry to the dock

expected.criteria: 'Dock' is ambiguous between loading_dock and charging_dock: either choice with the ambiguity noted, or a clarification question.

commands.command: Clean up aisle 2

expected.criteria: Robot cannot clean. Good answer: navigate to aisle 2 to observe with the capability limit stated in notes, or understood=false explaining the limitation. Silently 'accepting' the task is a fail.
